Xi Jinping:
Biography of Xi Jinping

1. Birth Details:
Xi Jinping (birth: 15 September 1953) was born in the Fuping district of Shaanxi province of China. His father Xi Jinshang was a communist leader, while mother Xi Jinming was a teacher. The people of their family came from rural areas, who had played an important role in the history of China’s political development.

2. Education:
Xi Jinping obtained a bachelor degree in chemistry from Shaanxi University. During his education, during the Cultural Revolution (1966-1976) in China, he was sent to a rural area, where he spent life with farmers. This experience forcefully made strong the importance of rural development and social justice in his political ideas.

3. Major Political Roles:

1975-1982: Remained district Communist Party secretary of the Fuping district of Shaanxi.
1982-1985: Remained deputy secretary of the state Communist Party of Fujian province, where he implemented policies of economic development and renewal.
1985-1992: Remained chairman of the state Communist Party of Jingjing and Jingjiang province, where he developed policies for economic expansion and renewal.
1992-1998: Remained chairman of the state Communist Party of Shanghai, where he implemented urban development and economic policies.
1998-2002: Remained chairman of the state Communist Party of Jingjing province, where he developed policies for economic development and renewal.
2002-2007: Remained chairman of the state Communist Party of Jingjing province, where he developed policies for economic development and renewal.

Barack Obama:
Biography of Barack Obama

1. Birth Details

Barack Hussein Obama Jr. was born on August 4, 1961, in the city of Honolulu, Hawaii. His father, Barack Hussein Obama Sr. (a Kenyan individual), and his mother, Ann Dunham (an American individual), were his parents. His father belonged to the Luo tribe of Kenya, while his mother was of Irish and English descent. Obama’s father was a teacher in Kenya, while his mother was an American teacher. His father was studying at a university in Kenya when his mother was studying at a university in Hawaii. His father and mother married in 1961, but two years after Obama’s birth, his father left Hawaii and returned to Kenya. Obama was raised in childhood by his mother and his uncle.

Dilma Rousseff:
Biography of Dilma Rousseff

1. Birth Details:

Dilma Rousseff was born on January 13, 1947, in the city of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Her father was a teacher, while her mother was a homemaker. Her brother was a lawyer, who later continued to assist her in her political life.

2. Education:

Dilma obtained a law degree from the National University of Brazil (UFRJ). During her education, she developed an interest in politics and social studies, which played an important role in shaping her political character.

3. Major Political Roles:
Entry into social movements and politics: In the 1980s, Dilma participated in social movements such as the National Council for Public Economics (CNPq), where she expressed her views on issues of poverty and education.
Mayor of Rio de Janeiro (2009–2010): In 2009, she was elected as the Mayor of Rio de Janeiro, where she focused on the development of the city and expansion of public facilities.
Prime Minister (2011–2016): In 2010, she was elected as the Prime Minister of Brazil, where she advanced social programs such as "Bolsa Família," which plays an important role in addressing poverty. She also promoted environmental protection through the "Green Brazil" program.
